learning_sequence_context.position_in_sequence: Lesson 2 of 10 in the Term 1-2 sequence - standalone NGRT reading assessment, between the Digital Literacy lesson and the start of the 8-lesson PowerPoint unit
learning_sequence_context.previous_lesson_summary: Last lesson pupils learned lab rules and the basic digital skills needed to work in the CS room (logging in/out, locking/unlocking, OneNote, OneDrive).
learning_sequence_context.next_lesson_link: Next lesson begins the PowerPoint unit with Mind Maps, starting the 'About me' presentation golden thread for the term.

expected_outcomes.lesson_objective: Pupils complete the New Group Reading Test (NGRT) under formal test conditions, providing a standardised baseline measure of reading ability to inform teaching and support across the course.
assessment_criteria: Each student will log in to the NGRT platform using their own account.
Each student will complete the test independently and silently within the given time.
Each student will submit their completed test through the platform.
expected_outcomes.activity_summary: Whole-class administration of the standardised NGRT reading assessment under formal test conditions; no new subject content is taught this lesson.

key_vocabulary.definition: New Group Reading Test - a standardised assessment used to measure reading ability.
To supervise pupils during a formal test to ensure fair and quiet conditions.

preparation.additional_adult_role: Circulate quietly to resolve technical issues (e.g. login problems) without disturbing pupils who are mid-test.
accessibility: Extra time/access arrangements applied per individual pupil plans, as for any formal assessment

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Explain the purpose of the NGRT (a standardised reading assessment, not a pass/fail test) and the formal test conditions: work silently and independently, raise a hand for technical issues, remain seated once finished.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Listen to the briefing and ask any clarifying questions about the test format or conditions before it begins.

check_for_understanding.question: If you finish early, what should you do?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Proceed to the test once the class can correctly state the quiet-finish procedure; otherwise repeat that part of the briefing.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Launch the NGRT test for the class on the administration platform and invigilate silently for the duration, resolving only technical issues.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Log in to the NGRT platform with their own account and complete the test independently and silently within the allotted time.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Confirm all pupils have submitted their test, close the platform, and briefly explain what the results will be used for (informing future support, not a grade).
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Confirm their test has been submitted and pack away.
